_README: Baseline allowlist for scripts/check-tenant-isolation.ts. Each entry is a route file (path relative to apps/unified-portal) that the heuristic currently flags: it queries a tenant-scoped table via a service-role client but contains no recognizable scoping token (tenant_id / developer_user_id / assertCanAccessDevelopment / requireRole(['super_admin']) / verified-ownership marker '// tenant-scope:'). The checker exits non-zero only when a NEW file is flagged that is not listed here. 2026-06 remediation: all HOLE entries from the audit have been fixed (requireRole + session-tenant scoping / ownership checks) and drained from this list; the remaining entries are verified-safe false positives or report-only items explicitly left untouched (homeowner/lookup-code, projects/create org ownership). 'classification' records the audit verdict.

allow.reason: Agent-profile scoped via getUser + agent_profiles.user_id; reads developments by client activeDevelopmentId without an ownership check (minor cross-tenant development-name read).

False positive: RLS-bound session client (createServerSupabaseClient), getUser auth, intelligence_actions filtered by developer_id = user.id; developments read derived from the user's own actions (ownership chain).
Pre-auth login flow; resolves a unit by login code to issue a session. No session exists yet by design.
Liveness probe; counts document_sections only. No tenant data of substance, excluded from middleware matcher by design.
Accepted token pattern: the unit_uid handover code IS the scope. (Minor: returns purchaser_name on a valid code; codes should retain entropy.) Report-only per 2026-06 remediation scope.
Registration claim flow keyed on the unit id from the prior lookup-code step; first-claim-wins, rejects already-claimed units.
Authenticated via getAdminContextFromSession; creates a project/units under a client-supplied requestedOrgId — verify org ownership is enforced. Report-only per 2026-06 remediation scope.
False positive: OAuth/magic-link callback uses the user's RLS-bound session client (createRouteHandlerClient + exchangeCodeForSession), not a service-role client; units read/update governed by RLS.
